name: ETLAS-2 found no blood-pressure difference with tadalafil in CSVD and more adverse events
statement: In older CSVD patients treated with tadalafil 20 mg daily for 3 months, ETLAS-2 reported no blood-pressure difference versus placebo, lower adherence, and more adverse events with tadalafil.
population_or_context: Patients with cerebral small vessel disease and previous stroke or transient ischemic attack; mean age 68.
quantitative_note: Adverse events occurred in 76% with tadalafil versus 36% with placebo; the abstract states no differences were observed in blood pressure.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports no blood-pressure difference, lower adherence, and higher adverse-event frequency.

caveats: 20 mg daily dose is higher than common 5 mg daily ED/BPH regimen.
population_profile: Older CSVD patients with prior stroke/TIA; low directness for healthy men age 30 to 50.
endpoint_measure: Blood pressure, adherence, and adverse events.
effect_estimate: No blood-pressure difference reported in abstract; adverse events 76% with tadalafil versus 36% with placebo.
safety_findings: Higher adverse-event frequency and lower adherence with tadalafil.
directness_to_target_population: Low for healthy men age 30 to 50 due to age, CSVD, prior stroke/TIA, and dose.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Feasibility trial, secondary BP endpoint, older disease population, and adherence imbalance.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; transfer limit is CSVD population and high daily dose.
